title: Align madreperla.yaml to canonical shape
body:
Fix madreperla.yaml to match the canonical shape used across all madreperla-managed repos.

## Changes

1. Fix field names: `docs:` → `usage:` (on both `providers.issues` and `providers.docs`)
2. Add path keys under `providers.docs`: `planning`, `epics`, `ideas`, `headaches`, `retro`
3. Remove `designs` key if present
4. Update `description` to: "Self-testing feedback loop for AI coding tools"

## Target state

```yaml
description: "Self-testing feedback loop for AI coding tools"
docs: [AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/]
providers:
  issues:
    name: pearls
    config: "prl configure"
    usage: "prl docs"
  docs:
    name: repo
    usage: "cat docs/README.md"
    planning: docs/planning
    epics: docs/planning/epics
    ideas: docs/planning/ideas
    headaches: docs/planning/headaches
    retro: docs/planning
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs:` fields renamed to `usage:` in both providers
- [ ] All path keys present: planning, epics, ideas, headaches, retro
- [ ] description updated
- [ ] `madp configure --provider claude` succeeds after change

title: Create AGENTS.md with agent-facing instructions
body:
Create a top-level AGENTS.md file with concise instructions for AI coding agents working in this repo.

## Content to include

- Repo purpose: what git-dogfood does (one sentence)
- Key files/directories: .dogfood/, install.sh, resolve script, dogfood.yml workflow
- How to test changes: any relevant test/validation commands
- Contributing conventions: commit message format, branch naming
- Pointer to madreperla.yaml for project configuration

Keep it concise — this is a reference card, not documentation. Target under 50 lines.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md exists at repo root
- [ ] Contains repo purpose, key files, testing, and conventions
- [ ] Under 50 lines
- [ ] Listed in madreperla.yaml `docs` array (already is)

title: Audit root README.md for accuracy
body:
Scan the existing README.md (74 lines) for stale or inaccurate content.

## Areas to verify

- Does the "How it works" flow diagram still match the actual workflow?
- Are prerequisites (git-vendored, git-semver) still accurate with correct URLs?
- Does the install command still work / point to the right path?
- Is the configuration section (per-vendor config JSON) still accurate?
- Are token requirements still correct?
- Does the resolve script still scan `.vendored/configs/*.json` with `.vendored/config.json` fallback?

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each section of README.md verified against current code
- [ ] Any inaccurate content corrected
- [ ] If everything is accurate, issue closed with a note confirming audit passed

title: Create docs/README.md table of contents
body:
Create docs/README.md as a table of contents for the docs/ directory.

## Content

List all docs/ subdirectories and files with brief descriptions:

- docs/planning/ — active planning docs and epics

Add entries as the docs/ directory grows. This file is referenced by madreperla.yaml (`providers.docs.usage: "cat docs/README.md"`).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] docs/README.md exists
- [ ] Lists all current docs/ contents with descriptions
- [ ] Serves as the entry point referenced by madreperla.yaml
